title: eremophila scaberula
intro: Eremophila scaberula, also known as the small-leaved emu bush, is a small shrub native to Western Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, and its leaves contain iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eremophila scaberula include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from skin irritations. Eremophila scaberula can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, making it a popular remedy among natural health practitioners.
